snowplow__atomic_schema.title: Schema
snowplow__atomic_schema.description: Schema (dataset) that contains your atomic events
snowplow__atomic_schema.longDescription: The schema (dataset for BigQuery) that contains your atomic events table.

snowplow__database.title: Database
snowplow__database.description: Database that contains your atomic events
snowplow__database.longDescription: The database that contains your atomic events table.

snowplow__dev_target_name.title: Dev Target
snowplow__dev_target_name.description: Target name of your development environment as defined in your `profiles.yml` file
snowplow__dev_target_name.longDescription: The [target name](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/core/connect-data-platform/profiles.yml) of your development environment as defined in your `profiles.yml` file. See the [Manifest Tables](/docs/modeling-your-data/modeling-your-data-with-dbt/package-mechanics/manifest-tables/) section for more details.

snowplow__events.title: Events Table
snowplow__events.longDescription: This is used internally by the package to reference your events table based on other variable values and should not be changed.

snowplow__start_date.title: Start Date
snowplow__start_date.longDescription: The date to start processing events from on first run or a full refresh, based on `derived_tstamp`.
snowplow__start_date.description: The date to start processing events from on first run or a full refresh

snowplow__allow_refresh.title: Allow Refresh
snowplow__allow_refresh.longDescription: Used as the default value to return from the `allow_refresh()` macro. This macro determines whether the manifest tables can be refreshed or not, depending on your environment. See the [Manifest Tables](/docs/modeling-your-data/modeling-your-data-with-dbt/package-mechanics/manifest-tables/) section for more details.

snowplow__backfill_limit_days.title: Backfill Limit
snowplow__backfill_limit_days.longDescription: The maximum number of days of new data to be processed since the latest event processed.
snowplow__backfill_limit_days.description: The maximum number of days of new data to be processed since the latest event processed

snowplow__hash_identifiers.title: Hash Identifiers
snowplow__hash_identifiers.longDescription: When set to `true`, all identifier values stored in `snowplow_identities_identifier_mapping` are hashed using SHA-256 before being written to the table. Recommended when identifiers contain PII.
snowplow__hash_identifiers.description: Hash all identifier values with SHA-256 before storing them (recommended for PII compliance)

snowplow__identifiers.title: Identifiers
snowplow__identifiers.longDescription: The list of identifiers to extract from events and link to each `snowplow_id`. Each entry has a `reference` (the column name in your events table) and an `alias` (the name used in the output tables). Add any event-level fields you want to track as identifiers, such as `email` or `phone`.
snowplow__identifiers.description: > Click the plus sign to add a new entry

reference.title: Reference
reference.description: Column name in the events table

alias.title: Alias
alias.description: Name to use in the output tables

snowplow__license_accepted.title: License accepted
snowplow__license_accepted.longDescription: Use this field to accept the Snowplow Personal and Academic License or specify that you have a commercial agreement with Snowplow.
snowplow__license_accepted.description: Use this field to accept the Snowplow user license.

snowplow__app_id.description: > Click the plus sign to add a new entry
snowplow__app_id.title: App IDs
snowplow__app_id.longDescription: A list of `app_id`s to filter the events table on for processing within the package.
